title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: In recent years, psychologists have become increasingly interested in the subtle ways in (0) people shape one another’s behaviour.||One area of particular focus is the phenomenon of social mirroring, (9) which refers to the tendency humans have to imitate gestures, expressions or even emotional states without consciously realising it. Researchers argue that such imitation is not merely accidental but a mechanism deeply rooted in our evolutionary past, one (10) that enabled early communities to strengthen cooperation.||Although the process is largely automatic, there are circumstances in which individuals find themselves at (11) a loss when trying to interpret the intentions of others, especially in unfamiliar cultural settings. In fact, it comes as no surprise that misunderstandings are (12) far more common in groups that share little in common.||Seldom (13) do scientists encounter a behavioural pattern as universal as mirroring, which appears across cultures and age groups alike. It is, by far, (14) one of the most reliable indicators of social bonding, albeit one that remains difficult to measure precisely.||Not only does mirroring influence how we perceive others, but it can also determine how relationships end (15) up forming. Provided (16) any person in a group initiates a positive gesture, the rest often follow, reinforcing a cycle of connection that shapes human interaction in profound ways.

example.text: In recent years, psychologists have become increasingly interested in the subtle ways in (0) people shape one another’s behaviour.

questions.explanation: Relative pronoun used to introduce a non-restrictive relative clause. After a comma, only 'which' is acceptable (not 'that').
Relative pronoun used to introduce a restrictive relative clause modifying 'one/mechanism'.
Part of the fixed idiomatic expression 'at a loss,' which means to be uncertain or confused.
Used as an intensifier in the phrase 'far more,' which emphasizes a significant difference in degree.
An auxiliary verb is required for negative inversion. When a sentence starts with a restrictive word like 'Seldom,' the subject and verb must flip.
A quantifier needed to complete the superlative structure 'one of the most...'
Completes the phrasal verb 'end up,' which describes the final state or result of a process.
Indefinite determiner used in conditional clauses meaning 'whichever' or 'if even one'. The fixed structure 'Provided any + noun' expresses a general condition.
